name: Black Bread
recipeInstructions:
Makes 2 loaves
1. Sprinkle yeast and sugar over the warm water; stir to dissolve. Let stand until foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. Melt chocolate and margarine with 1 1/4 cups water in a large bowl set over gently simmering water. Stir until smooth. Remove from the heat. Blend in molasses, vinegar, and salt. Mix in cereal. Let cool.
3. Grease a large bowl. Blend yeast into the cereal mixture. Gradually stir in 2 cups all-purpose flour and rye flour. Turn dough out onto lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes, kneading in up to 3/4 cup more all-purpose flour if necessary to form a workable dough.
4. Add dough to the prepared bowl, turning to coat the entire surface. Cover and let rise in a warm draft-free area until doubled in volume, about 2 hours.
5. Grease two 3x2x7-inch loaf pans. Punch dough down. Turn out onto lightly floured surface and let rest 3 minutes. Knead for 3 minutes. Divide dough into halves.
6. Roll each half into a 7x8-inch rectangle. Starting with the long side, roll the dough up into a cylinder. Tuck ends under and pinch the seam to seal. Place seam side down in prepared pans. Cover and let rise in a warm draft-free area until doubled, about 1 1/2 hours.
7. Preheat oven to 375 F. Bake until loaves sound hollow when tapped on bottom, about 45 minutes. Remove bread from pans. Let cool completely on a rack before serving.
